channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: US eggs prices hit a record high of $4.95 and are likely to keep climbing
item.description: OMAHA, Neb. (AP) — Egg prices hit a record high as the U.S. contends with an ongoing bird flu outbreak, but consumers didn't need government figures released Wednesday to tell them eggs are terribly expensive and hard to find at times.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Saudi educator known for charity and prisoner work wins $1 million Global Teacher Prize
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — A Saudi educator known for his charity work and instructing prisoners won the $1 million Global Teacher Prize on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL and MALAK HARB, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Why people are naming bugs, rats and cats after their exes this Valentine’s Day
item.description: ANCHORAGE, Alaska (AP) — Feel like dissing instead of kissing your former lover this Valentine’s Day? Think your ex is more like a rat than a prince? Do you believe your former paramour should never procreate? Animal shelters and zoos around the country are encouraging little cathartic avenues for r
item.dc:creator: MARK THIESSEN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: 7-year-old girl flown to Utah hospital after Wyoming mother shoots 4 daughters, herself
item.description: A 7-year-old girl was flown to a Utah hospital with a gunshot wound to the head after a Wyoming mother reportedly shot her four daughters before turning the gun on herself, according to the Big Horn County Sheriff's Office.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: MD, Baltimore mayor target Glock for selling pistols that can be converted into machine guns
item.description: The Maryland Attorney General and mayor of Baltimore City filed a lawsuit against Glock, Inc. for continuing to sell handguns that can be converted into machine guns.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Sean ‘Diddy’ Combs sues NBC over new documentary as he awaits trial on sex trafficking charges
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Sean ‘Diddy’ Combs is suing NBC Universal over a documentary that he says falsely accuses him of being a serial murderer who had sex with underage girls as he awaits trial on federal sex trafficking charges.
item.dc:creator: PHILIP MARCELO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Convicted CEO fraudster Elizabeth Holmes says life in lockup is 'hell' in first prison interview
item.description: Notorious Theranos founder Elizabeth Holmes, once celebrated as the first female self-made billionaire, broke her silence in her first interview from a federal Texas prison.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Republican lawmakers urge Trump to respect federal court rulings
item.description: GOP lawmakers are growing increasingly worried about the nation plunging into a constitutional crisis if Trump ignores court orders halting his most aggressive actions.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Military plane crashes near San Diego: authorities
item.description: Emergency crews are responding to reports of a military plane that crashed into the San Diego Bay near Shelter Island, according to the San Diego Fire-Rescue Department Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: FBI says it found 2,400 new JFK assassination records
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — The FBI on Tuesday said it discovered 2,400 new records related to the assassination of President John F. Kennedy as federal agencies work to comply with President Donald Trump's executive order last month to release thousands of files.
item.dc:creator: JAMIE STENGLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Gabbard awaits Senate vote on director of national intelligence nomination
item.description: Her chances of confirmation increased when Republicans fell in line following a pressure campaign by Trump allies.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Gangs not only ones preying on migrant women, boys
item.description: The road from Central America to the U.S. border is paved with dangers for vulnerable migrants often traveling with children.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, NewsNation

item.title: Education report reveals which states have seen biggest drops in reading, math scores
item.description: While some schools are starting to claw back some of the ground lost due to pandemic-era learning disruptions, but there's still a ways to go, a new report finds.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Ozzy Osbourne's 'final show' with original Black Sabbath: What we know
item.description: The performance, called "The Back to the Beginning," will cap off an all-day event featuring sets from several iconic heavy metal acts.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Langenfeld, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Cause of deadly Arizona plane crash revealed, deceased pilot identified
item.description: A landing gear failure caused the private jet owned by Vince Neil – the lead singer of Mötley Crüe – to crash into a parked private jet on Monday, killing its 78-year-old co-pilot.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 14 bands and artists nominated for Rock & Roll Hall of Fame
item.description: Mariah Carey, Chubby Checker, Cyndi Lauper, OutKast and Phish are some the 2025 nominees for induction into the Rock & Roll Hall of Fame, a list that also includes two sets of musical brothers who have had public feuds and recent reunions — the Black Crowes and Oasis.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Republican lawmakers urge Trump to respect federal court rulings
item.description: GOP lawmakers are growing increasingly worried about the nation plunging into a constitutional crisis if Trump ignores court orders halting his most aggressive actions.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Russia releases imprisoned American teacher Marc Fogel
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Marc Fogel, an American history teacher who was deemed wrongfully detained by Russia, has been released and returned to the U.S. in what the White House described as a diplomatic thaw that could advance negotiations to end the war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN and ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Storm makes for treacherous travel as snow and ice track through the mid-Atlantic
item.description: A snowstorm blew into the mid-Atlantic states Tuesday, causing dozens of accidents on icy roads, prompting school closures and stoking worries about possible power outages. The heaviest snowfall — up to 10 inches (25 centimeters) — was expected in parts of Virginia and West Virginia.
item.dc:creator: BEN FINLEY and JOHN RABY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: With public broadcasters pressured by the Trump administration, PBS shuts down its diversity office
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — PBS says it is shutting down its office of diversity, equity and inclusion to comply with President Donald Trump's executive order, firing the two executives brought on when the effort was begun in 2021.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Virginia residents outsmarted home invaders, stole their getaway car: prosecutor
item.description: Dogs really are a man's best friend – In Norfolk, Virginia one even provided a distraction that allowed a home invasion victim to escape.
item.dc:creator: Kenzie Finch, KRON4

item.title: Trump tariffs rattle small business owners already dealing with tight margins
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — President Donald Trump's continued roll out of a wide array of tariffs is rattling small business owners already dealing with tight profit margins. Trump on Monday announced a 25% tariff on steel and aluminum and promised more import duties to come.
item.dc:creator: MAE ANDERSON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Disney, amid changes to DEI initiatives, will alter content warning ahead of 'Peter Pan,' 'Dumbo' and other older films
item.description: Disney has become the latest company to change its diversity, equity and inclusion (DEI) initiatives as pressure from the federal government to eliminate such programs continues.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KRON4

item.title: A pilot on a private jet owned by Mötley Crüe singer killed in Arizona aircraft collision
item.description: SCOTTSDALE, Ariz. (AP) — One of two pilots on a private jet owned by Mötley Crüe singer Vince Neil died after the aircraft veered off a runway in Arizona and hit a business jet, according to a Federal Aviation Administration incident report.
item.dc:creator: SEJAL GOVINDARAO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump ally Steve Bannon pleads guilty and avoids jail time in border wall fraud case
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Steve Bannon pleaded guilty on Tuesday to defrauding donors to a private effort to build a wall on the U.S. southern border, ending a case the conservative strategist decried as a “political persecution.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Durbin calls for investigation into Patel's role in FBI firings
item.description: Sen. Dick Durbin (D-Ill.) is asking the Department of Justice’s (DOJ) inspector general to investigate “highly credible information” that Kash Patel, President Trump’s pick to head the FBI, is already directing a “purge” of the bureau before he’s been confirmed by the Senate.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Crested Butte Mountain Resort and union reach tentative agreement
item.description: The Crested Butte Mountain Resort and the Crested Butte Lift Maintenance Union say they reached a tentative agreement amid contract negotiations.
item.dc:creator: Maddie Rhodes, ABC 4

item.title: Private jets collide at Scottsdale Airport in Arizona, killing at least 1 person, authorities say
item.description: At least one person was killed and others were injured when private jets collided Monday afternoon at the Scottsdale Airport in Arizona, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trader Joe's is now limiting egg sales nationwide
item.description: Trader Joe’s patrons nationwide have taken to social media to comment on a new policy that limits the amount of eggs shoppers can buy. The company told Nexstar's KOIN that limits are currently in place at all of its U.S. locations.
item.dc:creator: Jashayla Pettigrew, KTLA 5

item.title: Member of Kendrick Lamar's Super Bowl halftime show won't face charges for flag protest
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — The cast member who displayed the Palestinian flag with the word “GAZA” in protest during Kendrick Lamar's Super Bowl halftime show will not face charges, New Orleans police said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: HIV infections could jump over 6 times if US support dropped & not replaced, UNAIDS chief says
item.description: The head of the U.N. AIDS agency said Monday the number of new HIV infections could jump more than six times by 2029 if American support of the biggest AIDS program is dropped, warning that millions of people could die and more resistant strains of the disease could emerge.
item.dc:creator: MARIA CHENG AP Medical Writer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Eagles running back Saquon Barkley sets the NFL's season rushing record, including the playoffs
item.description: Saquon Barkley set an NFL single-season rushing record in the Super Bowl, held his young daughter as a torrent of confetti fluttered around them and flashed a smile as he held a Philadelphia newspaper with “CHAMPS!” stamped above his photo.
item.dc:creator: DAN GELSTON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Eagles deny the Chiefs a Super Bowl three-peat with dominant defense in a 40-22 rout
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Yo Philly: Jalen Hurts and Nick Sirianni are world champions. A ferocious Philadelphia Eagles defense tormented and frustrated Patrick Mahomes while Hurts made all the plays the offense needed. So much for the Kansas City Chiefs’ quest for a Super Bowl three-peat.
item.dc:creator: ROB MAADDI, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Taylor Swift booed by heavily pro-Eagles crowd at the Super Bowl
item.description: Taylor Swift was back at the Super Bowl to watch Travis Kelce and his Kansas City Chiefs play the Philadelphia Eagles on Sunday. Her reception was a whole lot different than last year.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Here's what we know about a commuter plane crash in Alaska that killed 10 people
item.description: JUNEAU, Alaska (AP) — Authorities are focusing on the wreckage of a plane that crashed off western Alaska as they try to determine what caused the small commuter aircraft to go down in the icy Bering Sea, killing 10 people.
item.dc:creator: BECKY BOHRER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump says 25 percent tariffs on steel and aluminum coming Monday
item.description: Trump said that the steel and aluminum tariffs would impact “everybody” when asked what countries would be affected.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WGN 9

item.title: LeBron James will sit out the Lakers' game against Indiana to rest his sore left ankle
item.description: LeBron James is sitting out the Los Angeles Lakers ' game against Indiana on Saturday to rest his sore left ankle.
item.dc:creator: GREG BEACHAM, Associated Press, Fox 5
